mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate below 5% signals an overheated economy with limited slack, anticipating a recession and thus negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of UNRATE level vs 20-day forward returns is not consistently negative (IC > +0.03 over any 2-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, tight monetary policy depresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, the 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (mean > -0.5% or t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated and unemployment is low simultaneously, the economy is in a late-cycle phase with high risk of downturn, causing negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The combination of high rates and low unemployment does not produce more negative 5-day returns than the average of periods excluding this condition.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in industrial production indicate an industrial recession that leads to lower corporate earnings and negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month moving average declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: In trading months following trigger, the 60-day forward return is not negative in at least 50% of cases, or average return is not statistically below zero.

mechanism_narrative: The absolute level of the federal funds rate inversely predicts equity returns; higher rates compress P/E multiples, so a high current rate implies negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC of FEDFUNDS level vs 5-day forward returns is not consistently negative (IC > -0.03 over any 2-year window).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high inflation and high policy rates signal a stagflationary environment where both discount rates and inflation erode real equity returns, leading to negative 20-day returns.
failure_condition: During trigger periods, SPX 20-day returns are not systematically negative (mean return >= 0 or IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment begins to rise after a period of lows, it signals labor market deterioration and a likely recession, causing negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month moving average increases by 0.2 percentage points or more
failure_condition: After trigger, the proportion of positive 20-day forward returns is not below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts below a threshold indicates a severe housing downturn that spills over to the broader economy, predicting negative forward returns.
failure_condition: In the 20 trading days following trigger dates, S&P 500 return is not lower than in non-trigger dates by at least 1% on average.
